page.content: The first table presents the reference, structure of fiber, coating material, sensitivity, sensing range, and response time for various optical fibers. The entry for "This paper" lists a twisted macro-bend coupling structure with agarose coating, a sensitivity of 4.23 nW/%, a sensing range of 40–90%, and a response time of 1 second. The reference [13] features a singlemode polymer fiber Bragg, with no coating, a sensitivity of 0.23 mV/%, a sensing range of 10–90%, and a response time of 4.5 seconds. Reference [14] details a side polished fiber with WS2 film, a sensitivity of 0.1213 dB/%, a sensing range of 35–85%, and a response time of 1 second. The side polished fiber in reference [15] utilizes reduced graphene oxide, exhibiting a sensitivity of 0.31 dB/%, a sensing range of 70–95%, and a response time of 5 seconds. Reference [16] describes a tapered POF with HEC/PVDF, showing a sensitivity of 0.023 mV/%, a sensing range of 50–85%, and no response time reported.

lines.text: The first table presents the reference, structure of fiber, coating material, sensitivity, sensing range, and response time for
various optical fibers. The entry for "This paper" lists a twisted macro-bend coupling structure with agarose coating, a
sensitivity of 4.23 nW/%, a sensing range of 40–90%, and a response time of 1 second. The reference [13] features a
singlemode polymer fiber Bragg, with no coating, a sensitivity of 0.23 mV/%, a sensing range of 10–90%, and a response time
of 4.5 seconds. Reference [14] details a side polished fiber with WS2 film, a sensitivity of 0.1213 dB/%, a sensing range of 35–
85%, and a response time of 1 second. The side polished fiber in reference [15] utilizes reduced graphene oxide, exhibiting a
sensitivity of 0.31 dB/%, a sensing range of 70–95%, and a response time of 5 seconds. Reference [16] describes a tapered POF
with HEC/PVDF, showing a sensitivity of 0.023 mV/%, a sensing range of 50–85%, and no response time reported.

page.content: The second table outlines risk factors with corresponding β-coefficient and P-value. Age (per year) has a β-coefficient of 0.02 (95% CI: 0.006 to 0.027) and a P-value of 0.003; BMI (per kg/m²) shows a β-coefficient of 0.03 (95% CI: 0.02 to 0.04) with a P-value of <0.0001; creatinine has a β-coefficient of 0.002 (95% CI: −0.001 to 0.005) and a P-value of 0.18; time (per year) has a β-coefficient of −0.03 (95% CI: −0.06 to −0.01) and a P-value of 0.01; and HOMA-%S Q1 vs. Q2-4 shows a β-coefficient of −0.04 (95% CI: −0.13 to 0.06) with a P-value of 0.5.
